- To the right: WWWWB (white area at cols 20-24)
- Row 19: all B's (wall below)
So the blue block is now in a chamber at rows 14-18, cols 14-18. To the right is a white area at cols 20-24. Let me try RIGHT to move into that area.
Also, I notice the maze structure seems to have passages between chambers. The blue block moves 6 cells per step in the direction pressed, jumping to the next chamber.
Let me map out the maze chambers:
1. Top-left: rows 8-12, cols 14-18 (starting position)
2. Top-right: rows 8-12, cols 20-24 (reached by RIGHT from start)
3. Middle-left: rows 14-18, cols 14-18 (current position, reached by DOWN from start)
4. Middle-right: rows 14-18, cols 20-24 (white area to the right of current)
5. Large area: rows 14-38, cols 32-38
6. Bottom area: rows 49-55, cols 43-49 (blue target?)
Let me try RIGHT to move to the middle-right chamber.
